title: Long-Term Potentiation Explained
script_summary: Exploring how repeated activity strengthens brain synapses, revealing a key learning mechanism.

segments.voiceover: Did you know your brain can strengthen connections just by repeating actions?
This process is called Long-Term Potentiation, or LTP.
When you repeatedly activate a synapse, it becomes more sensitive, enhancing the signal.
Research shows LTP can last for hours, sometimes even longer.
Think about it: every time you practice, you're literally rewiring your brain.
Want to learn more about your brain? Hit follow for more neuroscience insights!
